problem.title: Power of Four
problem.description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of four. Otherwise, return false. An integer n is a power of four if there exists an integer x such that n == 4^x. Try to solve it without using loops or recursion.
assumptions: n can be negative or zero (return false).
Try to solve with O(1) bit manipulation.

example.explanation: 16 = 4^2. Three conditions: (1) n > 0, (2) n is a power of 2 (n & (n-1) == 0), (3) the single set bit is in an odd position (n & 0xAAAAAAAA == 0, masking even-position bits).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Bit Manipulation
solutions.description: A power of 4 satisfies three properties: (1) n > 0, (2) only one bit set — n & (n-1) == 0 (power of 2), (3) that bit is at an even position (0, 2, 4, ...) — n & 0xAAAAAAAA == 0 (the mask 0xAAAAAAAA covers all odd bit positions). Combining these three checks gives O(1) solution.
solutions.code_python:
def is_power_of_four(n: int) -> bool:
    return n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0 and (n & 0xAAAAAAAA) == 0

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Math with Log
solutions.description: n is a power of 4 if log base 4 of n is an integer. Use log(n) / log(4) and check if it is close to a whole number.
